title: osteomeles anthyllidifolia var. subrotunda
intro: Osteomeles anthyllidifolia var. subrotunda, also known as the 'Kauai rose', is a rare and endemic plant species native to the island of Kauai in Hawaii. It is a shrub with fragrant white flowers and has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in digestion. In traditional Hawaiian herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be made into teas, poultices, or infusions to leverage its medicinal properties.
